summary: color-style-utils ships postinstall scripts that collect host data and execute system commands
details: The package ships two install-time scripts (postinstall.js and postinstall2.js) that combine child_process execution with HTTP requests and system data collection. postinstall.js requires child_process and issues outbound http.request calls (GET and POST) alongside repeated hostname collection across multiple call sites (lines 20, 46, 287, 409, 427). postinstall2.js requires child_process, makes outbound http.request/https.request and POST calls, and invokes execSync('netsh...') — a Windows network-configuration command — at line 652. The combination of child_process execution, hostname/system-info gathering, and outbound HTTP traffic during the npm postinstall lifecycle hook fires automatically on `npm install` without consent and matches a host-reconnaissance / exfiltration dropper shape rather than any documented benign install-time behavior for a package advertising color/style utilities. There is no native source tree, no pinned vendor binary fetch, and no publisher-matching CDN that would justify the lifecycle network + exec activity.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
